---
title: "PROCSI C Training"
subtitle: "Two-Week Summer Camp, Summer 2026"
author: "Dan Negrut"
date: 2026-07-06
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    width: 1250
    margin: 0.08
    transition: fade
    footer: "PROCSI C Training  |  UW-Madison"
    embed-resources: true
---

## How the camp works {.smaller}

- Each **morning**, a short brief: today's topic, your target, and where to start. Then good luck.
- You work in **teams**, hands-on, all day. **Claude is your always-on tutor.**
- Late afternoon (**3:30 to 4:30**), each team gives a friendly **~10-minute** talk: how far you got and what you learned.
- End every talk with your team's **quote or joke of the day**.
- **No grades, no quizzes.** Discover new things, make new friends, and enjoy a little friendly competition.

## Teams

- We pick **5 captains**. Captains take turns (round-robin) drafting teammates into **5 teams**.
- **New teams each day**, so you keep meeting new people.
- Teams compete good-naturedly; at day's end, everyone **votes for a favorite**.
- The only stakes are bragging rights and fun.

## Before you start

- Bring a **laptop** (at least one per team is plenty).
- Create a free **Onshape** account: it runs in the browser, nothing to install.
- We install **Miniforge (conda)** and **PyChrono** together, no prior setup needed.
- Curiosity required; experience not.

# Week 1

## Week 1 at a glance

| Day | Focus |
|-----|-------|
| Mon, Jul 6  | Welcome, College of Engineering, meet everyone |
| Tue, Jul 7  | Command line and bash; meet Claude |
| Wed, Jul 8  | Python (conda) and a little git |
| Thu, Jul 9  | Blender: render your first image |
| Fri, Jul 10 | Onshape mechanism, then render it in Blender |

## Monday, July 6

- CSULA cohort arriving late
- [AM, 1153ME]{.am} College of Engineering at UW-Madison; how the camp works
- [PM, 1153ME]{.pm} Get to know each other; draft the first teams
- [PM]{.pm} Ice cream expedition at Memorial Union

## Tuesday, July 7

- [AM]{.am} The command line and bash: files, folders, navigation; `git clone` as just another command
- [AM]{.am} Meet Claude, your tutor for the whole camp
- [PM]{.pm} Hands-on in teams; ~10-minute talks and the first quote or joke of the day

## Wednesday, July 8

- [AM]{.am} Install Miniforge (conda); Python basics: variables, loops, functions, arrays
- [AM]{.am} Just-enough git: `add`, `commit`, `push` to save your work
- [PM]{.pm} Python exercises with Claude; team talks

## Thursday, July 9

- [AM]{.am} Blender basics; render a still image
- [PM]{.pm} Teams render a scene; team talks
- [PM]{.tour} Lab tour

## Friday, July 10

- [AM]{.am} Onshape: build a simple mechanism (slider-crank or four-bar); export
- [PM]{.pm} Import the CAD part into Blender, set a scene, render; team talks

# Week 2

## Week 2 at a glance

| Day | Focus |
|-----|-------|
| Mon, Jul 13 | What does "simulate" mean? The sliding block and forward Euler |
| Tue, Jul 14 | Turn the math into a Blender movie of the block |
| Wed, Jul 15 | PyChrono: the same block for real, plus wow demos |
| Thu, Jul 16 | AI-assisted modeling with RAG (chrono-rag) |
| Fri, Jul 17 | Showcase, vote, and State Street |

## Monday, July 13

- [AM]{.am} The sliding block: mass m, force f, over 10 s; the equation of motion; forward Euler
- [PM]{.pm} Teams code the Euler loop in Python and plot position vs time; team talks
- [PM]{.tour} Lab tour

## Tuesday, July 14

- [AM]{.am} From numbers to motion: turn the Euler output (a CSV) into a Blender keyframe animation
- [PM]{.pm} Teams render their block movie over a real backdrop; team talks

## Wednesday, July 15 {.smaller}

- [AM]{.am} Install PyChrono (conda); the **same block**, now with real contact and friction
- [AM]{.am} Two demos: a slider-crank mechanism, and a rover on terrain
- [PM]{.pm} Teams run and tweak a demo; team talks
- [PM]{.tour} Lab tour

## Thursday, July 16

- [AM]{.am} What is RAG? Use chrono-rag to generate a PyChrono model, then run it
- [PM]{.pm} Bigger, still-friendly team showcase; team talks

## Friday, July 17

- [AM]{.am} Final friendly showdown; students vote for a favorite team; group photo
- [PM]{.pm} Head to State Street and have fun. Leave early.
